name: Sunflower Seeds
summary: One of the richest whole-food sources of vitamin E, with magnesium and healthy unsaturated fats.

benefits.claim: Top source of vitamin E for skin and cells
benefits.mechanism: Sunflower seeds are among the most vitamin-E-dense foods. Vitamin E is a fat-soluble antioxidant that protects cell membranes — including skin cells — from oxidative damage.

citations.title: Vitamin E — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

benefits.mechanism: Alongside vitamin E, sunflower seeds provide magnesium — needed for muscle, nerve, and bone health — and mostly unsaturated fats that support healthy cholesterol when they replace saturated fat.

citations.title: Magnesium — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

surprisingFacts.text: A single ounce of sunflower seeds covers roughly half a day's vitamin E — one of the most concentrated food sources of the vitamin.

cautions.effect: Calorie-dense enough that large daily handfuls add up quickly, and a big jump in fibre and fat can unsettle your stomach. Nothing toxic at food amounts. Salted versions add up on sodium.
